food-01.googleReviewSummary: The pistachio escargot, sacristain and croissants are worth the hype at this gorgeous 1875-decor bakery; complaints cite long lines, brusque cashier service and weekend closures.

food-02.googleReviewSummary: Exceptional macarons with inventive flavors like the Ispahan and helpful staff; complaints focus on high prices and occasionally dismissive service for small purchases.

food-03.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers love the baba au rhum and classic pastries in the gorgeous 1730 interior; occasional gripes about tourist crowds, prices and curt counter service.

food-04.googleReviewSummary: The legendary sourdough miche, punitions cookies and apple tarts are celebrated; a recurring complaint is cold or haughty service.

food-05.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers adore the famously thick L'Africain hot chocolate and Mont-Blanc in the Belle Époque salon; complaints cite long queues, high prices and rushed, touristy service.

food-06.googleReviewSummary: The intensely flavored sorbets and salted-caramel ice cream are called the gold standard in Paris, though some find scoops small and pricey with long waits.

food-07.googleReviewSummary: The generously stuffed falafel pita with eggplant and harissa is called the best in Paris and great value; complaints mention long lines, chaotic crowds and brusque, rushed service.

food-08.googleReviewSummary: Authentic buckwheat galettes, Brittany ciders and friendly staff at fair prices; complaints mention waits without reservations and cramped seating.

food-09.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers love the lively, authentic atmosphere and variety of French, Moroccan, Lebanese, Italian and Japanese stalls, though it gets very crowded at lunch with limited shared seating.

food-10.googleReviewSummary: Customizable sandwiches on crispy bread with cheese tastings from the charismatic Alain himself; the dominant complaint is queues of 30-60+ minutes.

food-11.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers single out the perfectly cooked steak au poivre and warm old-school hospitality; complaints center on hard-to-get reservations (phones go unanswered) and uneven starters.

food-12.googleReviewSummary: The Provençal dishes, charming terrace and famous help-yourself chocolate mousse bowl are loved; complaints cite long waits, tightly packed tables and inattentive service when busy.

food-13.googleReviewSummary: Unbeatable cheap traditional French menu and lively Belle Époque hall; complaints mention long queues, rushed waiters and canteen-level food quality.

food-14.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers come for the iconic literary history, people-watching terrace and good hot chocolate, but say the food is mediocre and very overpriced with indifferent service.

food-15.googleReviewSummary: Pancakes with bacon and bourbon butter, excellent specialty coffee and friendly English-speaking staff; main complaint is long weekend brunch queues since there are no reservations.

food-16.googleReviewSummary: Expertly curated natural wines, top-tier small plates and knowledgeable, unpretentious staff; main gripe is the cramped standing-room space that fills up fast.

food-17.googleReviewSummary: Cult natural wine list and simple, excellent nibbles like zested broad beans in a shoebox-sized space; a recurring complaint is occasionally brusque service.

food-18.googleReviewSummary: The aged comté and truffled brie are called among the best cheese in France with knowledgeable pairing advice, though some report impatient staff with hesitant customers.

food-19.googleReviewSummary: Silky caramels, fresh-mint chocolates and thick hot chocolate in the elegant Marais salon; the only recurring complaint is very high prices.

food-20.googleReviewSummary: Sublime Basque bistronomie, the legendary riz au lait and boisterous convivial atmosphere; main complaints are extremely tight seating and noise.

tour-01.googleReviewSummary: The night-time sparkle and observation-deck views are unforgettable; reviewers repeatedly warn about long security lines, dense crowds, and pickpockets around the base.

tour-02.googleReviewSummary: An incomparable, essential art collection; the recurring complaint is overcrowding around the Mona Lisa and the museum's exhausting size.

tour-03.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers rave about the breathtaking post-fire restoration, stained glass, and free entry; the main complaint is very long entrance queues since reopening.

tour-04.googleReviewSummary: Free entry, glittering mosaics, and panoramic city views; complaints about heavy crowds, the steep climb, and pushy vendors outside.

tour-05.googleReviewSummary: The rooftop panorama — especially at night when the Eiffel Tower sparkles — is worth it; gripes about the 284-step climb and confusion over the underground access tunnel.

tour-06.googleReviewSummary: World-class Impressionist collection in a stunning former railway station; main complaint is crowds and waits at the most famous rooms.

tour-07.googleReviewSummary: The 15 floor-to-ceiling stained-glass windows are called the most beautiful in Europe and photos don't do them justice; frequent complaints about chaotic entry lines even with timed tickets.

tour-08.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers gush over the opulent grand staircase, gilded interiors, and Chagall ceiling; occasional complaints that the auditorium is closed for rehearsals without warning.

tour-09.googleReviewSummary: Monument views from the water and good value, with dinner cruises praised for food and live music; complaints target the loud multilingual audio commentary and slow boarding.

tour-10.googleReviewSummary: The Hall of Mirrors and gardens are jaw-dropping and worth a full day; the dominant complaint is extreme crowds and long queues even with advance tickets.

tour-11.googleReviewSummary: The bone-lined tunnels are an eerie, unforgettable experience with a good audio guide; complaints about tickets selling out, the steep price, and the demanding stairs.

tour-12.googleReviewSummary: The modern art collection and rooftop views are praised; recent frustration centers on the museum's closure for multi-year renovation.

tour-13.googleReviewSummary: Immaculate lawns, fountains, and iconic green chairs make a peaceful escape; weekend overcrowding is the main complaint.

tour-14.googleReviewSummary: The charming cobbled square full of working portrait artists is enjoyed, but reviewers repeatedly note it is extremely touristy with overpriced restaurants.

tour-15.googleReviewSummary: The stroll from the Arc de Triomphe toward Concorde is iconic, especially lit up at night; it feels commercialized and crowded with real pickpocket risk.

tour-16.googleReviewSummary: The massive dome, Foucault's pendulum, and the moving crypt of Voltaire and Marie Curie are praised, with only mild complaints about queues and limited dome-access slots.

tour-17.googleReviewSummary: The single best photo spot for the Eiffel Tower with lively fountains; warnings about sunset crowds, aggressive street vendors, and pickpockets.

tour-18.googleReviewSummary: The French Revolution history and Marie Antoinette's cell are fascinating, especially with the HistoPad AR tablet; some feel the largely empty halls need that context.

local-01.googleReviewSummary: The laid-back tree-lined quays, iron footbridges and picnic spots are loved; complaints cite litter and scruffier stretches near Stalingrad.

local-02.googleReviewSummary: A peaceful elevated garden walk above the city — the original High Line — with lush plantings; complaints mention crowded narrow sections.

local-03.googleReviewSummary: Dramatic cliffs, lake, waterfalls, the Temple de la Sibylle and skyline views; gripes about sections closed for maintenance and heavy weekend crowds.

local-04.googleReviewSummary: Cheap fresh produce, authentic local atmosphere and friendly vendors offering samples; the small flea-market section is underwhelming and weekends get very crowded.

local-05.googleReviewSummary: The panoramic belvedere view over Paris (great at sunset) and terraced gardens are praised; the steep climb is tough and secluded paths can feel dodgy alone.

local-06.googleReviewSummary: The pastel-colored car-free street is charming, calm and very photogenic; a recurring complaint is that it's a 2-minute visit and overhyped.

local-07.googleReviewSummary: The 1799 covered passage's historic charm, glass roof and atmospheric bistros are loved; it gets cramped at peak meal times and some shops are cash-only.

local-08.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers cherish the legendary cramped literary atmosphere and stamped books as souvenirs; complaints are long entry queues and no photos allowed inside.

local-09.googleReviewSummary: Well-maintained lawns, lake and a peaceful oasis feel ideal for picnics and jogging; minor complaints about crowds on sunny days.

local-10.googleReviewSummary: The eco-friendly converted train station with its urban farm and good-value organic menu is loved; complaints cite slow service and hit-or-miss food.

local-11.googleReviewSummary: Movie-set tropical decor and well-priced cocktails in a hidden canal-side venue; arrive early to avoid queues, and food/service get inconsistent when busy.

local-12.googleReviewSummary: A charming village-like quarter with cobbled lanes, street art and lively cheap bars; noisy weekend nightlife and quiet shuttered streets by day.

local-13.googleReviewSummary: 'The mother of all flea markets' with endless vintage treasures; complaints cite high prices (more antique mall than bargain flea) and a sketchy stretch near the metro.

local-14.googleReviewSummary: A wild, off-the-beaten-path walk along the abandoned railway with surprising biodiversity; complaints about graffiti, scruffy upkeep and limited access points.

local-15.googleReviewSummary: The treasure-hunt thrift digging and famous 1-euro bins yield genuinely cheap vintage finds; recurring complaints target rude staff and chaotic piles.

local-16.googleReviewSummary: The quirky house-café concept (you can sit in a bathtub), canal views and good brunch are adored; complaints cite slow service and a worn interior.

exp-01.googleReviewSummary: The single best postcard view of the Eiffel Tower, especially at sunrise and during the night sparkle; consistent warnings about crowds, pushy vendors and pickpockets.

exp-02.googleReviewSummary: A tranquil tree-shaded tip of Île de la Cité — romantic picnic and sunset spot with Seine views on both sides; summer crowds on the small lawn are the main gripe.

exp-03.googleReviewSummary: Patient, funny English-speaking chefs and hands-on small classes where you take home everything you bake; virtually no complaints beyond classes selling out.

exp-04.googleReviewSummary: The breathtaking glass dome and free rooftop terrace with panoramic views are praised (rating is for Galeries Lafayette Haussmann overall); the dominant complaint is overwhelming crowds.

exp-05.googleReviewSummary: The free guided tour on perfume history with generous scent testing is appreciated; some find it feels like a sales funnel into the Fragonard boutique.

exp-06.googleReviewSummary: The Féerie show's costumes, dancers and acrobatics are spectacular; repeated complaints about cramped seating, mediocre dinner and high prices.

exp-07.googleReviewSummary: A mesmerizing, artistic light-and-dance show with elegant service in an intimate room; complaints center on expensive drinks and some restricted-view seats.

exp-08.googleReviewSummary: The historic vaulted cellar, live jazz and swing-dancing crowd are 'worth every euro'; it gets extremely hot, cramped and crowded once the band starts.

exp-09.googleReviewSummary: Near-unanimous 5-star reviews call the vintage sidecar ride a trip highlight with charming, knowledgeable drivers; tours run rain or shine so dress for weather.

exp-10.googleReviewSummary: The green riverside book boxes embody the literary soul of Paris; many stalls are shut on Sundays and some now sell mostly trinkets. (Google listings are fragmented; rating approximate.)

exp-11.googleReviewSummary: The stunning white basilica, free entry and one of the best panoramic views in Paris; main complaints are heavy crowds and the steep climb — which is exactly why sunrise is the move.

exp-12.googleReviewSummary: Rated the best rooftop terrace in eastern Paris for sunset views and atmosphere; recurring complaints cover entry lines, slow service and elevated prices.

exp-13.googleReviewSummary: A huge canal-side park packed with free activities, themed gardens, the science museum and open-air cinema; parts feel run-down and events get crowded.

exp-14.googleReviewSummary: Reviewers adore the old-fashioned riverside guinguette atmosphere with dancing and the rowboat crossing; the rating is dragged down by pricey average food and slow service.

exp-15.googleReviewSummary: Attending a ballet or opera under the Chagall ceiling is magical; complaints limited to restricted-view cheap seats and tickets selling out.

exp-16.googleReviewSummary: A hauntingly beautiful, peaceful place to wander among famous graves and ornate tombs; poor signage makes graves hard to find and the cobbled hills need good shoes.

exp-17.googleReviewSummary: Early visitors praise the well-organized setup (lifeguards, lockers, showers, two big pools) and the novelty of swimming in the Seine; complaints include murky water, peak-time queues and water-quality closures.

exp-18.googleReviewSummary: A small authentic Left Bank market praised for high-quality produce and cheese in a very Parisian atmosphere; stalls pack up by mid-afternoon.
